data.title: Chin Hin’s 1Q net profit doubles on contribution from Signature International, turnaround in property development
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Chin Hin Group Bhd (KL:CHINHIN) saw its first-quarter net profit double, mainly on contributions from kitchen and wardrobe maker Signature International Bhd (KL:SIGN) and a turnaround in its loss-making property development segment.

Faster progress in in-house property projects and better construction billings also helped raise profits for the quarter ended March 31, 2025.

Net profit for the quarter under review rose to RM18.39 million from RM9.07 million in the same period a year earlier, according to the diversified group’s bourse filing on Thursday. Earnings per share was almost flat at 0.52 sen versus 0.51 sen a year ago.

Signature International’s better margins boosted Chin Hin’s overall gross profit margin to 18.61%, up from 10.57% last year. The property segment made a RM26.27 million profit, reversing a RM0.98 million loss. While the construction segment also improved, supported by in-house property projects and better billing from current contracts.

Despite lower revenue, the building materials segment saw a 49.9% profit increase, mainly due to better performance in distribution, precast concrete and metal roofing, supported by cost-cutting efforts.

Revenue for the quarter under review was 66.9% higher at RM951.95 million compared to RM570.21 million in 1QFY2024.

No dividend was declared for the quarter.

The group, in a filing with Bursa Malaysia, said as of March 31, 2025, Signature International had a strong order book of RM907 million for kitchen and wardrobe systems and RM322 million for interior fit-out works. The company expects better revenue and profit in FY2025, supported by ongoing and new orders. 

Chin Hin also expects the construction business, which had an outstanding order book of RM1.8 billion, to see steady contract flow, supported by upcoming in-house property projects. It plans to continue bidding for new contracts.

The property development segment had unbilled sales of RM2.1 billion from ongoing projects like Quaver Residence, Ayanna Resort Residences, Avantro Residences, and others. It also plans to launch new projects in the Klang Valley and Southern Region in 2025.

Chin Hin said its building materials division is under pressure from weaker demand, rising costs, and more competition, which have hurt revenue and margins. However, it remains profitable due to strong demand for key products like autoclaved aerated concrete, precast concrete and drymix, along with ongoing efficiency improvements.

Shares in Chin Hin closed one sen or 0.5% lower at RM2.14, valuing the group at RM7.58 billion. The counter has declined 9.70% year-to-date.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: IHH Healthcare’s 1Q net profit down 33% due to exceptional accounting adjustments
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): IHH Healthcare Bhd (KL:IHH) saw its net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) drop 33% to RM514 million from RM768 million a year earlier, primarily due to a lower net monetary gain resulting from accounting rules under MFRS129, as well as the absence of last year’s deferred tax credit from asset revaluation in Türkiye.

Excluding these exceptional accounting adjustments, the group’s core net profit — after tax and minority interest — increased by 5% year-on-year (y-o-y) in 1QFY2025. Earnings per share for 1QFY2025 came in lower at 5.83 sen compared with 8.72 sen for 1QFY2024.

Revenue for the quarter, however, grew 5.7% to RM6.29 billion from RM5.96 billion a year earlier, driven by stronger contributions from its operations in Malaysia, Türkiye and Europe, supported by improved inpatient volumes.

IHH Healthcare did not declare any dividend for the quarter.

Despite robust demand for quality healthcare, IHH Healthcare said it acknowledged ongoing sector-wide challenges, including rising cost pressures, notably higher energy and staff expenses.

The group’s staff costs increased 11% y-o-y to RM2.52 billion while finance costs rose 10% to RM282 million, mainly reflecting higher interest expenses related to recent acquisitions such as Island Hospital and Agilus.

In response to rising costs, IHH Healthcare has launched a multi-year transformation plan focusing on seven key areas: clinical excellence, patient experience, new care models, operational efficiency, payor and regulator engagement, employee and doctor value propositions, and the advancement of technology, data and artificial intelligence.

This strategic initiative aims to accelerate growth and future-proof the business, it said.

Acknowledging persistent payor pressures in many of its markets, the group said it is actively collaborating with regulators and payors to manage the challenge of rising medical inflation.

“Our highly diversified healthcare portfolio, spanning multiple geographies, provides significant business resilience. This geographical spread allows us to buffer the impact of pressures in some countries with the strong performance of others, giving us a key strategic advantage,” it added.

Shares of IHH Healthcare closed unchanged at RM6.91 on Thursday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM60.97 billion. The counter has fallen over 5% year to date.

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: NationGate 1Q profit more than doubles on strong data segment, declares 25 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): NationGate Holdings Bhd (KL:NATGATE) saw its net profit more than doubled in the first quarter, lifted by strong demand from its data computing segment.

Net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025) came in at RM53.9 million or 2.37 sen per share, compared with RM21 million or 1.01 sen per share a year earlier, as revenue surged nearly 14 times to RM2.84 billion from RM207 million.

However, the group’s gross profit margin declined to 3.6% in 1QFY2025, from 16.5% a year ago, due to high key component costs in the data computing segment.

NationGate declared an interim dividend of 0.25 sen per share, unchanged from 1QFY2024, with the payment date to be announced later.

On a quarter-on-quarter basis, net profit fell 15.9% from RM64.1 million in 4QFY2024, hurt by lower sales, foreign exchange losses, asset write-offs, and fair value losses on derivative financial instruments.

Revenue also dipped 6.4% from RM3.03 billion, due to lower contribution from the data computing segment.

Despite ongoing geopolitical tensions and trade disputes, NationGate said it remains optimistic about future prospects, particularly in the electronics manufacturing services industry.

It expects continued growth driven by the shift of manufacturing operations out of China, technological advancements, digital transformation, and rising interest from data centre providers.

Heavy selling ahead of results

Nevertheless, shares in NationGate tumbled in active trade on Thursday ahead of the results announcement, triggering Bursa Malaysia Securities’ suspension of intraday short selling.

The counter fell as much as 26 sen or 16.5% to RM1.32 in the afternoon session. Intraday short selling will be halted when the last price of an approved security falls below the daily limit — 15% for stocks priced above RM1, and 15 sen for those under RM1.

At the closing bell, NationGate pared some losses to finish at RM1.44, still down 14 sen or 9%, giving the company a market capitalisation of RM3.28 billion. Year to date, the counter has plunged over 42%.

More than 118 million shares changed hands — nearly triple its 90-day average volume — making it the second most actively traded stock on Bursa Malaysia for the day.

The reason behind the heavy selling pressure was not immediately clear. Analysts remain bullish, with five out of six covering the stock maintaining a "buy" call and one with a "hold". The consensus 12-month target price stands at RM2.13.
 

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: UEM Edgenta sees 1Q net loss as 'temporary blip', expects rebound in 2Q
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Asset management and infrastructure solutions outfit UEM Edgenta Bhd (KL:EDGENTA), which slipped into the red in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), is anticipating a turnaround as early as the second quarter of this year, supported by the group’s cost-saving measures and improved operational efficiencies.

The group posted a net loss of RM17.95 million in 1QFY2025, compared with a net profit of RM9.77 million a year earlier. The last time UEM Edgenta slid into the red was in 3QFY2020 due to pandemic-related disruptions in its infrastructure business and inventory impairments.

UEM Edgenta chief financial officer Ahmad Fazril Fauzi views the 1QFY2025 net loss as a “temporary blip,” attributing it to seasonal factors such as festive holidays that slowed down activities during the quarter.

“Typically, the first quarter is slow — it was a perfect storm of all these factors contributing to our temporary blip,” he said during the group’s financial results briefing on Thursday.

“We are confident that the net loss in 1QFY2025 was merely a temporary blip. Starting from 2QFY2025, we expect to see a rebound, and a return to the earnings and profitability levels previously achieved,” he added.

It posted a loss per share of 2.16 sen in 1QFY2025 compared with earnings per share of 1.17 sen in 1QFY2024.

Revenue for the quarter fell 4.7% to RM646.06 million from RM677.58 million a year earlier on higher operational costs, particularly in manpower, as well as the completion of several one-off contracts and cyclical factors.

UEM Edgenta Bhd, in which UEM Group Bhd holds a 69% stake, remains optimistic about its earnings outlook as the group is on track to achieve its RM150 million cost savings target for the financial years ending December 31, 2024 (FY2024) through FY2029. A total of 28.5% or RM42 million of these savings have been realised as of 1QFY2025.

“Regarding manpower costs, we are actively engaging with our customers to negotiate direct cost pass-through or minimum cost-sharing mechanism, and we have seen good success,” Ahmad Fazril explained.

He noted that cost savings will accelerate throughout the year, supported by initiatives such as workforce mechanisation, vendor consolidation and enhanced operational efficiency.

On dividends, the group reaffirmed its policy to pay out 50% to 80% of net profit and remains committed to delivering long-term shareholder value.

“We remain confident in the dividend payout, and as we expect to return to previously achieved profitability levels — which should occur within the next three quarters — we anticipate being able to distribute dividends to shareholders,” Ahmad Fazril said.

The group paid a dividend of four sen per share in FY2024, up from two sen per share in FY2023.

When asked about the group’s healthcare concession with the Ministry of Health, Ahmad Fazril said discussions are ongoing regarding potential revisions to service fees in response to minimum wage adjustments.

“The revenue impact would remain unchanged if terms stay the same, but we are still negotiating revisions to the service fee arising from the minimum wage changes,” he added.

UEM Edgenta recently secured a three-year extension of its concession agreement with the government to provide hospital support services at contract hospitals in Perak, Penang, Kedah, and Perlis.

The current agreement, which began on April 1, 2015, is set to expire on March 31, 2025. However, the group has not disclosed the value of the contract extension, as the agreement is yet to be signed.

Shares of UEM Edgenta, which fell 13% following the subdued 1QFY2025 results announcement on May 27, closed unchanged at 73.5 sen on Thursday, giving the group a market valuation of RM611.24 million.

Read also:
Khazanah-backed UEM Edgenta slips into the red in 1Q as revenue, margins weaken
UEM Edgenta's stock falls as much as 13% after quarterly loss
UEM Edgenta bags three-year extension to provide support services to govt hospitals in four states

Edited ByKang Siew Li

data.title: Jaya Tiasa’s 3Q net profit jumps fourfold on high palm product prices
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Jaya Tiasa Holdings Bhd (KL:JTIASA) reported a net profit of RM40.93 million for the third quarter of financial year 2025 (3QFY2025), more than four times higher than RM9.89 million a year earlier. This was due to higher selling prices of crude palm oil (CPO) and palm kernel. Earnings per share rose to 4.23 sen from 1.02 sen.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia, the company said revenue increased 16.7% for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 to RM244.54 million, helped by better sales in oil palm, timber, and other segments. Costs stayed about the same.

No dividend was declared for the quarter under review.

For the first nine months of FY2025, net profit grew 50.3% to RM187.77 million, with earnings per share rising to three sen. Revenue also increased 14.2% to RM903.63 million.

The group expects the palm oil market to stay weak due to lower soybean oil prices, seasonal higher CPO supply, and tariff issues affecting prices.

Jaya Tiasa said it plans to focus on sustainable resource management, improving efficiency, and boosting worker productivity to handle rising costs from wage increases and economic challenges.

Jaya Tiasa’s shares closed up 0.9% at RM1.16, with a market value of RM1.13 billion, though the stock is down 10.77% year-to-date.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Carlsberg Malaysia’s 1Q profit rises 7% on lower taxes, declares 23 sen dividend
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Bhd (KL:CARLSBG) reported a 7.49% increase in net profit for the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 (1QFY2025), largely due to the absence of a deferred tax liability recognised in the same period last year relating to foreign withholding tax in its Sri Lankan associate, Lion Brewery (Ceylon) PLC (LBCP).

The group posted a net profit of RM94.52 million for the quarter, up from RM87.93 million a year earlier. Earnings per share rose to 30.91 sen, compared with 28.76 sen in the previous year’s corresponding period.

The company declared a first interim single-tier dividend of 23 sen per share for the quarter under review, with payment scheduled for July 28. 

Despite the profit growth, quarterly revenue declined 8.67% year-on-year (y-o-y) to RM662.81 million, down from RM725.76 million in the same quarter last year.

In a filing with Bursa Malaysia on Thursday, the group attributed the decline to lower sales volume, which was impacted by the earlier timing of Chinese New Year in 2025. The high base effect from the previous year, when trade purchases were brought forward in anticipation of a price adjustment, also contributed to the y-o-y drop.

Revenue from the Singapore market decreased by 8.8% to RM168.2 million, which the group attributed to lower sales resulting from the earlier festive period and an appreciation of the ringgit against the Singapore dollar during the quarter.

Looking ahead, Carlsberg, the world's third largest brewer behind Anheuser-Busch InBev and Heineken, said it will continue its efforts to navigate the challenging macroeconomic environment, marked by external headwinds and increased uncertainty that may result in prolonged soft consumer sentiment. 

The group added that it will maintain focus on cost optimisation while supporting its strategic priorities, including brand premiumisation, product innovation and digital transformation, as part of its commitment to delivering sustainable value for shareholders and stakeholders.

Carlsberg’s shares closed six sen or 0.3% higher at RM19.56 on Thursday, valuing the group at RM5.98 billion. Year to date, the stock has retreated 5.32%.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: IJM Corp’s 4Q net profit falls almost 30% on credit loss from West Coast Expressway
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): IJM Corporation Bhd’s (KL:IJM) net profit for the fourth quarter was down almost 30% to RM128.95 million, mainly due to a RM54 million expected credit loss related to the West Coast Expressway. Last year’s profits included one-time gains, including a RM67 million fair value gain from WCE Holdings Bhd warrants.

IJM in its filing with Bursa Malaysia said that without these one-off losses, pre-tax profit would have risen 40.8% to RM311.4 million.

Revenue for the quarter ended March 31, 2025 (4QFY2025) was marginally higher at RM1.8 billion.

For the quarter, IJM said construction revenue grew 60.4% from more projects and a bigger order book. Its industry division also did well with strong orders and ongoing data centre and infrastructure work. The toll division was affected by the RM54 million loss, while its port business faced lower cargo volumes.

The company declared an interim dividend of five sen and a one sen special dividend, bringing the total payout to eight sen per share for the year.

For the full year ended March 31, 2025, net profit dropped 32% to RM403.4 million from RM600.3 million, while revenue increased to RM6.3 billion from RM5.9 billion.

Group CEO Datuk Lee Chun Fai said FY2025 was a year of progress despite economic challenges, with growth in construction, steady demand in industry and new wins in data centres, logistics and the UK. IJM’s order book currently stands at RM7.6 billion, plus RM3.5 billion from a new UK joint venture, boosting construction strength and its UK presence.

IJM said it expects growth in construction and industry, focusing on completing its RM7.6 billion order book and expanding data centre projects in FY2026. Property and toll businesses should provide stable income, while the port business faces challenges from global trade issues. The group remains confident despite economic uncertainty.

IJM’s share price closed 0.82% lower to RM2.43 on Thursday, giving it a market capitalisation of RM8.5 billion. Year to date, the stock is down 20%.

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Padini sees strong festive sales in 3Q, but bonuses trimmed gains
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Padini Holdings Bhd’s (KL:PADINI) net profit boasted a 77.6% increase compared to the year before, but gains were partially offset by a payout in employee bonuses.

The retail clothing company reported that it experienced higher sales during the Chinese New Year and Hari Raya festive season but continues to face headwinds, including weakened purchasing power amid global trade tensions, rising operational costs and inflation.

Padini’s net profit for the three months ended March 31, 2025 (3QFY2025) was RM71.97 million, up from RM40.5 million the year before, according to an exchange filing. Revenue for the quarter rose by 5.6% year-on-year to RM1.55 billion.

Padini declared a fourth interim dividend of 1.80 sen per ordinary share and a special dividend of one sen per ordinary share, payable June 2025.

To navigate market uncertainties, Padini said it is implementing cost-control strategies and efficiency measures, which includes conserving cash reserves, optimising working capital and streamlining operations to reduce potential negative impacts.

Despite ongoing headwinds, the company said in its report that it maintains a positive outlook for the latter half of the year.

Padini’s shares closed upy three sen or 1.5% to RM2.06 on Thursday, valuing the company at RM2.03 billion.

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Microsoft shares go from laggard to leader as AI growth improves — analysts
data.description:
(May 29): After languishing for months, Microsoft Corp shares are back within striking distance of a record high amid signs that performance in its Azure cloud-computing business is back on track. 

The software giant’s shares are about 2% shy of the record reached last July, with their 16% advance in May putting them on track for the best month in more than three years. The rally has been fueled by the broader rebound in US equities, as well as better-than-expected results from Azure, which investors are betting will continue as artificial intelligence (AI) drives more business.

“AI is becoming a bigger and bigger component of those revenues,” said Nancy Tengler, the chief executive officer of Laffer Tengler Investments. “I do think it’s a sustainer.”

The strength in Microsoft shares is a stark reversal from last year, when concerns about its position in the AI race and disappointing Azure growth weighed on the stock. Microsoft’s 12% advance in 2024 made it the worst performer among the so-called Magnificent Seven stocks, which also includes Apple Inc, Nvidia Corp, Alphabet Inc, Amazon.com Inc, Meta Platforms Inc and Tesla Inc. 

This year, Microsoft’s 9% advance has outperformed all of those peers save for Meta, which is up 10%. In addition to benefiting from limited exposure to US President Donald Trump’s tariffs, Microsoft emerged as one of the biggest winners from a mostly strong earnings season for Big Tech. 

The Redmond, Washington-based company reported revenue and profit in its fiscal third quarter that beat Wall Street estimates. Azure was the standout, with revenue growth of 33% exceeding expectations.  

Azure’s sales include both AI services — the computing power that backs OpenAI’s ChatGPT and similar tools — and other products like databases and on-demand storage. Microsoft chief financial officer Amy Hood said last month that it was those non-AI businesses that outperformed expectations in the most recent quarter. 

This week, TD Cowen analysts led by Derrick Wood projected that AI-related Azure revenue would rise to about US$24 billion in the 2026 fiscal year, from roughly US$4 billion in fiscal 2024. The analysts, who have a 'buy' rating for the stock, raised their price target to US$540 from US$490, implying a potential gain of about 18% from Wednesday’s closing price. Of the 72 analysts tracked by Bloomberg that cover Microsoft, only six have 'hold' ratings and none recommend selling.

“A return to Azure upside trends and firmer growth in the mid-30% level should help drive a stronger narrative around Azure and greater fund flows back into MSFT,” TD Cowen analysts wrote on Tuesday, referring to the company by its ticker symbol. 

At 30 times profits projected over the next 12 months, Microsoft is priced at a premium to the Nasdaq 100 Index and its own 10-year average, both of which sit around 26 times, according to data compiled by Bloomberg.

In the past week, Microsoft has traded sideways, which Tengler sees as a sign that the shares don’t have much more near-term upside.  

On the other hand, Kevin Walkush, a portfolio manager at Jensen Investment Management, expects the stock to continue moving higher, amid indications that the company will generate a growing share of revenue from AI services.

“Microsoft is probably one of the best long opportunities in AI,” said Walkush, whose firm, like Tengler’s, owns the stock. “Players like Microsoft tend to profit and do very well because they can kind of monetise the ongoing activity pretty continuously and tend to be very high-margin.”

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Salesforce raises annual sales outlook, suggesting AI payoff
data.description:
(May 29): Salesforce Inc raised its annual sales forecast, suggesting that its artificial intelligence (AI) agent product is on a path to contribute significant revenue.

The software company said revenue will be US$41 billion (RM173.39 billion) to US$41.3 billion in the year ending in January 2026, compared with an earlier forecast of US$40.5 billion to US$40.9 billion. 

Leading technology platforms such as Salesforce, Microsoft Corp and ServiceNow Inc are competing to offer AI agents — software that can complete tasks such as customer service without needing direction from a person. Salesforce launched its “Agentforce” product in October and is aiming for broad adoption among its customers.

Still, investors are waiting to see a more significant revenue contribution from Agentforce. The company said in a statement on Wednesday that it closed more than 4,000 paid deals for the product.

The company’s shares gained about 2% in premarket trading on Thursday after closing at US$276.03. 

Earlier this week, the San Francisco-based company announced it would spend about US$8 billion to buy Informatica, which focuses on organising and managing data in the cloud. AI implementation has been slowed in large companies because information is scattered and needs to be pulled together from many areas.

“Informatica is a data power play,” Robin Washington, Salesforce chief financial and operating officer, said in an interview. Along with other data-focused Salesforce products, Informatica will help customers implement AI tools sooner, she said. 

The Informatica buy fits Salesforce Inc chief executive officer Marc Benioff’s longtime strategy to use acquisitions as a way to spur revenue growth.

Annual recurring revenue for Salesforce’s division that includes data organisation and AI crossed US$1 billion in the period ended April 30, the company said. That’s up from US$900 million in the previous quarter and “points to consistent AI demand”, Anurag Rana, an analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence, wrote in a note after the results. 

The Informatica deal fits chief executive officer Marc Benioff’s longtime strategy to use acquisitions as a way to spur revenue growth. Benioff had paused that effort for the past few years under pressure from activist investors to cut expenses and improve profitability. Should the purchase be completed, it would be Salesforce’s biggest deal since it acquired office communications platform Slack for about US$27.7 billion in 2021.

Some analysts have expressed caution that a significant chunk of Informatica’s business is from customers running the software on their own servers rather than in the cloud, where Salesforce’s products are used. Washington said Salesforce will help accelerate Informatica’s cloud business.

Fiscal first-quarter revenue increased about 8% to US$9.8 billion. Remaining performance obligations, a measure of bookings, were US$60.9 billion. Profit, excluding some items, was US$2.58 per share. All those metrics exceeded Wall Street estimates.

As the imposition of US tariffs and other changing government policy roiled businesses over recent weeks, investors have debated how the swings will affect the software industry, which doesn’t directly face import taxes. Firms such as Microsoft have reported that customer behaviour hasn’t been noticeably impacted while others like Workday Inc have referenced an “uncertain environment”.

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Saudi Arabia seeks new IPOs to attract foreign investors
data.description:
(May 29): Saudi Arabia aims to increase potential public offerings, as it seeks to diversify its economy away from oil and develop its financial sector.

“We are doing almost 30% from last year,” said Mohammed Al-Rumaih, chief executive officer of the Saudi stock exchange, in an interview with Bloomberg Television, referring to the increase in numbers.

Asked about the pipeline for initial public offerings (IPOs) in the kingdom, Al-Rumaih said that like last year, they were from different industries. 

Saudi Arabia’s stock exchange saw about 15 firms list last year, with food and bevarage the top category. It has already seen companies raise more than US$1 billion (RM4.24 billion) this year. IPOs are a key part of the kingdom’s plan to diversify investment and draw in stronger revenues from outside the oil industry.

Al-Rumaih expects more listings from telecom and other tech companies in the future.

Uploaded by Liza Shireen Koshy

data.title: FBM KLCI down 0.3% to 1,518.98 on May 29, 2025
data.description: KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Below is a summary of how key Bursa Malaysia indices and the ringgit performed on Thursday:

data.title: NTT Docomo to buy Net Bank for US$2.9b in cut-throat Japanese market
data.description:
(May 29): NTT Docomo Inc is buying SBI Holdings Inc’s online bank in a ¥420 billion (US$2.9 billion or RM12.3 billion) deal to shore up the Japanese mobile carrier’s financial offerings in a hyper-competitive market.

Nippon Telegraph & Telephone Corp’s mobile unit will launch a tender offer for SBI Sumishin Net Bank Ltd, offering ¥4,900 per share — a 49% premium to the online bank’s closing price on Wednesday. At the conclusion of the deal, Docomo aims to own 65.81% of the online bank, which will delist. Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp will continue to hold 34.19% of SBI Sumishin, the companies said in a statement on Thursday.

Shares of SBI Sumishin Net Bank surged by a record 21.3% after local outlets including the Nikkei reported the news earlier. NTT’s stock price rose 0.6% while SBI Holdings gained 7.8%.

The move is part of a capital alliance between the state-backed telecommunications group and SBI Holdings, where NTT will invest ¥110 billion in the venture capital fund operator for an 8.25% stake.

NTT and SBI Holdings will co-develop new services from asset management to insurance, while NTT Data Group will provide support for SBI Holdings’ financial services, executives said. 

Docomo, which has close to half of Japan’s mobile phone market, has been slow to bolster its financial asset offerings and services compared with its rivals. That’s as nimbler competitors have gradually eaten into its dominant share.

Japan’s third-largest carrier SoftBank Corp made the country’s biggest payments app PayPay a subsidiary in 2022 and recently announced a three-way partnership with Sumitomo Mitsui Banking Corp’s credit card operations. 

No 2 mobile provider KDDI Corp has its own financial unit. Online shopping mall operator Rakuten Group Inc has started a mobile unit, seeking to expand the reach of its lucrative financial arms via the telecommunications business.

The acquisition targets existing Docomo customers rather than attract new ones and is a defensive move to prevent cancellations, said Naoki Fujiwara, a senior fund manager at Shinkin Asset Management. If the carrier can expand into areas like investment trusts, it may create additional revenue, he added.

Uploaded by Tham Yek Lee

data.title: Singapore’s central bank appoints National Arts Council chairman and Nanyang Technological University president to board of directors
data.description:
SINGAPORE (May 29): The Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS) has appointed Goh Swee Chen and Professor Ho Teck Hua to its board of directors. They will serve for a term of three years, from June 1 to May 31, 2028.

Goh is the chairman of the National Arts Council and the Nanyang Technological University (NTU) board of trustees. She is also on the boards of Singapore Airlines (SIA), JTC Corporation, Woodside Energy and GenZero.

Ho is the president of NTU and is an...(click on link for full story theedgesingapore.com)

data.title: Forex gain lifts Genting Malaysia's 1Q profit despite revenue slip
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Genting Malaysia Bhd's (KL:GENM) net profit rose 25.69% year-on-year (y-o-y) in the first quarter ended March 31, 2025 on the back of foreign exchange gains and improved cost management.

The group, which fell into the red in the fourth quarter last year, saw net profit rise to RM72.58 million or 1.28 sen per share, from RM57.78 million or 1.02 sen per share a year earlier.

Quarterly revenue slipped 6.14% y-o-y to RM2.6 billion from RM2.76 billion due to weaker contributions from its leisure and hospitality segments in Malaysia and the UK, as well as currency translation effects.

No dividend was announced for the quarter.

For the quarter, Genting Malaysia’s leisure and hospitality segment accounted for RM2.54 billion or 97.76% of group revenue. Its Malaysian operations contributed RM1.62 billion, down 7% y-o-y, mainly due to the timing of festive seasons and lower premium player volumes.

Revenue from the UK and Egypt dropped 7% to RM413.4 million due to adverse forex effects. Revenue from the US and Bahamas, meanwhile, fell 3% to RM501.3 million.

The group remains cautiously optimistic on its near-term prospects as "the regional gaming market may face increasing challenges" although the hospitality sector is still positive, it said.

It aims to enhance Resorts World Genting’s appeal through new ecotourism offerings and anniversary celebrations.

A key development includes the RM177 million acquisition of a remaining 51% stake in Genting Empire Resorts LLC, positioning the group for growth in New York’s gaming market, pending regulatory approval, it said.

"The group continues to closely monitor developments related to the New York Gaming Facility Board's Request for Applications, and is preparing to participate in the process as part of its broader growth strategy," it added. 

Edited ByAdam Aziz

data.title: Wellcall hits one-month low after 2Q miss, with weak exports and high costs expected to hurt earnings
data.description:
KUALA LUMPUR (May 29): Shares of Wellcall Holdings Bhd (KL:WELLCAL) fell to a more than one-month low after its disappointing second-quarter results. Analysts lowered forecasts and target prices for the stock, as they expect weaker exports, falling sales, and higher costs to persist.

Wellcall fell as much as 4.2% or six sen to RM1.38 on Thursday, its lowest since April 15. The stock rebounded off intraday lows and closed at RM1.42 after 370,800 shares exchanged hands. At the last price, the company had a market capitalisation of RM707 million.

Wellcall’s earnings for the second quarter ended March 31, 2025 (2QFY2025) fell short of expectations. Core net profit dropped to RM8.2 million, bringing total earnings for the first six months to RM21.5 million — down 15.5% year-on-year. This result came in at just under 40% of what analysts had expected.

Apex Securities cut Wellcall’s FY2025 and FY2026 earnings projections by 16.9% and 14.4% respectively in a research note on Thursday, and lowered its target price to RM1.72, pegging it to 17 times revised FY2026 earnings.

The firm attributed the downgrade to sluggish global manufacturing activity, margin pressure from an unfavourable USD/MYR exchange rate, on top of the weaker industrial hose demand. It noted that 90% of Wellcall’s sales are driven by exports.

Apex Securities said, however, that the rubber hose manufacturer’s planned production expansion remains on track, despite the fall in demand. Testing for a new mandrel line machine in Plant 3 is scheduled for June 2025, and is expected to boost capacity by 20% and enhance efficiency.

BIMB Securities cut Wellcall’s FY2025 earnings forecast by 13%, and also lowered estimates for FY2026 and FY2027 by 4.2% and 2.3% respectively. Its target price was reduced to RM1.62. Despite the cuts, it kept a 'buy' rating, citing steady customer orders, strong margins, solid cash reserves, and attractive dividends.

Similarly, Inter-Pacific Research lowered its earnings forecasts for Wellcall by 21.2% for FY2025, and 13.8% for FY2026, while also setting a target price of RM1.62 for the stock.

All three houses have 'buy' calls on Wellcall.

Read also:
Wellcall treads carefully with defensive strategy

Edited ByPresenna Nambiar

data.title: Hong Kong, Saudi bourses seek deeper ties to spur trading
data.description:
(May 29): Hong Kong and Saudi Arabia are ramping up efforts to boost trading activity, as inflows to their exchange-traded funds (ETFs) wane and cross-listings fail to materialise.

Hong Kong Exchanges & Clearing Ltd chief executive officer Bonnie Chan mentioned “increasing connectivity and opportunities” between the city, China and the Middle East, while speaking at the Capital Markets Forum on Thursday, which was co-hosted with the Tadawul stock exchange. Her counterpart at the Saudi bourse, Mohammed Al-Rumaih, said it is necessary to “enhance awareness” between both markets.  

Hong Kong and Riyadh have strengthened relations in recent years, as the Asian city seeks to attract rich Gulf families and diversify its investor pool. Meanwhile, the oil-rich kingdom has worked to raise foreign ownership and pump liquidity in publicly traded stocks under its Vision 2030 agenda, with Chinese investors seen as central to that plan.

An ETF, tracking bonds, issued by the Saudi government, started trading in Hong Kong on Thursday. It’s the latest offering to join a slew of ETFs tracking Saudi and Chinese shares that have listed in Hong Kong, Shenzhen, Shanghai and Riyadh since 2023, though trading volumes and inflows have remained slim since their debut.

“There seems to be limited organic and natural demand for these products, despite their strong performance, as most the assets under management seems to have come at inception with cornerstone investors, including Saudi Arabia’s Public Investment Fund (PIF) and the Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA),” said Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Rebecca Sin.

Hong Kong “aims to expand the connection with the Middle East market,” including through opportunities in bonds and derivatives, the city’s financial secretary Paul Chan said. “The government is taking a very proactive and conducive attitude in terms of policy facilitation.”

HKEX announced in October that it planned to open an office in the Saudi capital in 2025, as it sought to promote “greater connectivity between China and the Gulf region,” according to a statement. That same month, Hong Kong’s flagship carrier Cathay Pacific Airways Ltd started operating three return flights per week between the city and Riyadh. 

Hong Kong’s equity market has recovered sharply since chief executive John Lee’s visit to the Gulf in early 2023, as Beijing seeks to reinforce the city as a funding hub for its companies. Major new share sales returned to the HKEX this year, after a lackluster period, mostly boosted by offerings from big Chinese firms that are already listed in the mainland.

Earlier this month, electric-vehicle batteries giant Contemporary Amperex Technology Co, Ltd. raised US$5.3 billion (RM22.46 billion) in Hong Kong, following the world’s largest listing of 2025. Total proceeds raised through equities sales in the city have already reached the highest for a full year since 2021.

Hong Kong’s benchmark Hang Seng Index is up 17% this year, one of the best performers globally, contrasting with a drop of about 8% for the Tadawul All Share Index. A memorandum of understanding to explore cross-listings between the exchanges was signed in 2023, though none have been executed so far.

While there could be more ETFs listed across both regions this year following government-backed policies, “more resources and education will be needed to get investors excited to invest into them,” said Bloomberg Intelligence’s Sin. 

Uploaded by Liza Shireen Koshy

data.title: HP plunges in premarket trade after cutting profit outlook on tariffs, economy
data.description:
(May 29): HP Inc dropped about 10% in premarket trading on Thursday after the company’s profit outlook fell short of estimates and it cut the annual earnings forecast, pointing towards a weaker economy and continuing costs from US tariffs on goods from China.

Earnings, excluding some items, will be 68 cents (RM2.88) to 80 cents a share in the period ending in July, the maker of computers and printers said on Wednesday in a statement. Analysts, on average, estimated 91 cents. Fiscal second-quarter profit was 71 cents a share, compared with the average estimate of 81 cents. Profit was dented by 12 cents from the impact related to tariffs and HP’s spending to move manufacturing out of China, said chief financial officer Karen Parkhill. 

Demand for computers is being hurt by rising economic uncertainly tied to tariffs, the impact of which was greater than the company expected when it gave its earlier forecast, chief executive officer Enrique Lores said in an interview. The company is boosting production in Vietnam, Thailand, India, Mexico and the US. By the end of June, almost all products sold in North America will be made outside of China, he said. Still, the PC market will grow at a more moderate pace because of the slowing economy. 

HP chief executive officer Enrique Lores says clearly the economic environment is very different now from what it was in February and consumer and some business confidence has clearly changed.

HP reduced its annual adjusted profit outlook to US$3 to US$3.30 a share from a previous forecast of US$3.45 to US$3.75 a share.

“Clearly the economic environment is very different now from what it was in February and consumer and some business confidence has clearly changed,” Lores said. Apart from a weaker economy, industrywide price increases are hurting demand, he said. “We thought it was important to be prudent.” 

The stock closed at US$27.20 in New York and has declined 16% this year.

In the quarter ended April 30, revenue increased 3.3% to US$13.2 billion, slightly above the average estimate of US$13.1 billion. 

A recovery in the long-ailing personal computer (PC) market has started to materialise in recent quarters, but tariffs are derailing progress. Shipments of PCs ticked up 4.9% in the March quarter, according to IDC, an industry research firm. Some of that may be due to customers making purchases ahead of President Donald Trump’s tariffs announced on April 2, the market research firm said. 

Revenue in HP’s personal system business, which includes PCs, increased 7% to US$9 billion. Analysts, on average, estimated US$8.8 billion.

Lores said HP saw a “fairly small” impact from customers moving up purchases.

“We expect to fully mitigate the increased trade-related costs” by the fourth quarter, he said. 

Uploaded by Felyx Teoh

data.title: Markets ask how soon Nippon Steel will benefit from US$15b bid for US Steel
data.description:
TOKYO (May 29): Nippon Steel investors and analysts are asking if its US$15 billion (RM63.56 billion) deal to buy US Steel, backed but not yet approved by President Donald Trump, is positive for the near term, even if its hopes for strong US demand materialise.

Such a merger would create the world's third-largest steel producer by volume, after China's Baowu Steel Group and Luxembourg-based ArcelorMittal, data from the World Steel Association (WorldSteel) shows.

The "planned partnership" would create at least 70,000 jobs and add US$14 billion to the US economy via Nippon Steel's additional investments, Trump said last week.

While full details of the deal remain unclear, US Steel shares surged 21% on the news, and Nippon Steel gained 7%.

Nippon Steel did not exclude issuing new shares to fund the takeover, vice-chairman Takahiro Mori said in December, after having already raised some funds through hybrid financing and asset sales.

"If the new equity is issued, investors will rightly be asking: is this the best possible use of capital at this moment?" said Fiona Deutsch, lead analyst with Australasian Centre for Corporate Responsibility (ACCR).

The company had pledged an investment of up to US$4 billion in a new coal-dependent blast furnace, said Deutsch, whose climate activist group holds less than 1% of Nippon Steel's shares.

That plan, part of a wider investment commitment of US$14 billion, comes "at a time when the global steel sector is shifting towards low-carbon alternatives", she added.

Unveiling the deal in late 2023, Nippon Steel offered US$55 for each share of US Steel, for a premium of 40% at the time. US Steel shares closed at US$53.30 on Wednesday.

"There's a lot of immediate negative effects, even though the long-term effect may be positive," said an adviser to institutional investors on strategies for Nippon Steel.

The adviser cited the dilution as a further deterrent, apart from the high offer price and additional investment commitments.

Nippon Steel did not reply to a Reuters request for a comment.

"In the short term, there are concerns about financing," said Shinichiro Ozaki, a senior analyst at Daiwa Securities.

"Given that US Steel reported a net loss for the January-March period, the stock market may worry about the limited likelihood of a short-term return on the investment."

Shares of Nippon Steel were up 0.8% in Tokyo afternoon trade, underperforming a rise of 1.9% in the benchmark Nikkei index.

Strategic goals

Projections for domestic demand to stay weak have pushed Nippon Steel, which is Japan's largest steelmaker, and others to look to overseas expansion, while they consider shutting some blast furnaces at home.

US Steel is key to Nippon Steel's goal to raise its global output capacity to more than 100 million metric tons a year, from 63 million tons now, as it aims to benefit from demand in India and the United States.

Both markets are relatively protected from vast steel exports from China, the world's top producer, thanks to protectionist measures they have adopted, such as tariffs.

In March, Nippon Steel president Tadashi Imai, who also chairs the Japan Iron and Steel Federation, warned that US auto and steel tariffs could cut several million tons from Japan's annual steel output to below 80 million tons.

Ownership of US Steel could provide a shield for Nippon Steel from the impact of tariffs on non-US operations, said Alistair Ramsay, vice-president of Rystad Energy.

"Should underlying demand in the United States begin and continue to recover, then we would expect the investment to pay off in good time, regardless of the duration of tariffs," he said.

"But that's a big if, given how far the US market has shrunk over the past few years, never mind this century."

US steel consumption is expected to rise by 2% this year, after a drop of 1.5% in 2024, according to WorldSteel.

Trump is expected to talk about the deal during a rally on Friday at a US Steel plant in Pennsylvania.

If the deal goes ahead, US Steel will have an American chief executive and a majority-US board with a so-called "golden share" for the United States, conferring veto power over key decisions, a US senator has said.

This month, Nippon Steel said it would cut its dividend for the current fiscal year to 120 yen (US$0.824) a share, off last year's 160 yen, and its lowest since 2021, amid a projected fall in profits, but the overall payout ratio would stay at 30%.

"For the investor who cares about the share price today, you wouldn't be looking at factoring in synergies based on what you think might happen in two to three years," said the adviser, who sought anonymity as the matter is a sensitive one.

Uploaded by Liza Shireen Koshy

data.title: Stocks, dollar rally as Trump tariffs hit court roadblock
data.description:
LONDON/SYDNEY (May 29): European stocks and Wall Street futures rose on Thursday after a US federal court blocked President Donald Trump's so-called "Liberation Day" tariffs from going into effect, sending the dollar up on safe-haven currencies.

The little-known Manhattan-based Court of International Trade ruled that Trump overstepped his authority by imposing his April 2 across-the-board duties on imports from US trading partners.

The White House quickly appealed the decision, and could take it all the way to the Supreme Court if needed. But in the meantime, it offered some hope that Trump might back away from the highest tariff levels he had threatened.

"The ruling has brought a temporary sense of relief to the markets, even as uncertainty lingers over whether the administration will fully comply," said James Leong, chief executive officer at Grasshopper Asia.

"While volatility has eased for now, the lack of clarity around the government's response could reignite market turbulence. Until the Supreme Court provides a definitive ruling, we're unlikely to see a lasting resolution," he added.

The ruling could also encourage US trading partners to stall any trade negotiations they are having with the White House while they wait to see how the case is resolved.

However, analysts at Goldman Sachs noted the order does not block sectoral levies, and there were other legal avenues for Trump to impose across-the-board and country-specific tariffs.

"This ruling represents a setback for the administration's tariff plans and increases uncertainty but might not change the final outcome for most major US trading partners," analyst Alec Phillips wrote in a note.

Europe's STOXX 600 index was up 0.3% in early London trade.

US markets looked primed for a stronger reaction with S&P 500 futures last up 1.6%. Nasdaq futures were up 2%, also benefiting from relief over earnings from Nvidia, which beat sales estimates.

But Britain's FTSE 100 index shrugged off the news and was last down 0.1%.

"Is this a sign that stock markets in countries who did manage to score trade deals with the US in recent weeks, could be at a disadvantage if tariffs are reversed? This could be a short-term theme to watch," said Kathleen Brooks, research director at XTB.

Britain was the first country to secure a trade deal with the US and will hold talks with Washington next week to speed up the implementation of that deal, the Financial Times reported.

Earlier in Asia, Japan's Nikkei rose 1.9%, while South Korean shares climbed 1.9% to a nine-month high. Chinese blue chips firmed 0.6%.

Safe havens take a backseat

The news of the court decision hit traditional safe-haven currencies, which have benefitted from tariff fears punishing the US dollar.

The dollar gained nearly 1% against the Japanese yen. It later eased and but remained up 0.3%. The dollar was, meanwhile, up 0.4% against the Swiss franc.

Another beneficiary of dollar woes, the euro dropped as much as 0.7% and was last down 0.2% against the greenback.

US Treasury yields rose, adding to the pressure on the market unnerved by Trump's hefty tax and spend bill, which passed the House of Representatives last week.

Yields on 10-year Treasuries, which move inversely with prices, were up four basis points to 4.52% and markets further shaved the chance of a Federal Reserve rate cut anytime soon.

Longer-dated, 30-year yields held above the closely-watched 5% level.

Minutes of the last Fed meeting showed "almost all participants commented on the risk that inflation could prove to be more persistent than expected" due to Trump's tariffs.

A rate cut in July is now seen as around a 20% chance, while September has come in at around 60%, having been more than fully priced a month ago.

In commodity markets, gold was down 0.2% to US$3,283 an ounce.

Oil prices extended a rally first begun on supply concerns as Opec+ agreed to leave its output policy unchanged and the US barred Chevron from exporting Venezuelan crude.

Brent rose US$1 to US$65.9 a barrel, while US crude rose similarly to US$62.84 per barrel.

Uploaded by Magessan Varatharaja
